page: OVHcloud c2-30 is a compute-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) running at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration provides 8 dedicated physical cores with 8 vCPUs, meaning hyperthreading is disabled with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of memory, yielding 3.75 GB per core, and includes a bundled 200 GB SSD for local storage. The network configuration includes a baseline bandwidth of 0 Gbps and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics are relatively low, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with Geekbench multi-core scoring 4585.0 and stress-ng multi-core performance reaching 6708.13. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 682.04 GB/sec for cached small blocks, but write bandwidth for large blocks is weaker at 23.36 GB/sec.

This server is suited for parallelized workloads that can leverage its 8 dedicated physical cores, such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and basic database operations. It achieves average marks in Clang compilation and Redis SET operations. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or memory-write-heavy operations due to its single-core performance limitations. The dedicated CPU allocation provides stable execution without resource sharing, offering predictable compute density for medium-scale web serving and lightweight multi-threaded applications.
description: OVHcloud c2-30 is a compute-optimized server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is configured with an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) operating at 3.0 GHz, delivering 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs without hyperthreading. The system includes 30.0 GB of RAM (3.75 GB per core) and a 200 GB local SSD. Benchmark results show weak single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the bottom 10%, but average multi-core capabilities, as seen in its Geekbench multi-core score of 4585.0 and Passmark CPU score of 10085.08. Memory read bandwidth is average, while write bandwidth is weaker. This instance is appropriate for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, Redis database operations, and static web serving, where dedicated CPU resources provide consistent execution, though it is constrained in single-threaded processing efficiency.
og_description: OVHcloud c2-30 is a compute-optimized KVM server with 8 dedicated Intel cores, 30 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, delivering average multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c2-30 is a compute-optimized KVM server featuring 8 dedicated Intel cores, 30 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute instance with balanced multi-core performance and local SSD storage for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical Intel cores at 3.0 GHz with hyperthreading disabled
30.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 200 GB local SSD
Average multi-core performance for software compilation and database operations
Weak single-core CPU performance and memory write bandwidth
Suitable for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and Redis deployments
